description: Small unverified seed reference set derived from the internal reusable test portfolio. It is context for C1 matching and is not ground truth.

reference_mappings.requirement_example: Brake control shall detect brake pressure sensor failure within the required diagnostic time.
reference_mappings.reason: The test directly checks brake pressure sensor fault detection timing.

reference_mappings.requirement_example: Steering assist shall enter manual fallback after a steering angle sensor fault.
reference_mappings.reason: The test directly verifies manual steering fallback after sensor fault detection.

reference_mappings.requirement_example: The lighting controller shall use a safe default mode when ambient light input is unavailable.
reference_mappings.reason: The test verifies safe default lighting behavior after sensor input loss.

reference_mappings.requirement_example: Powertrain torque shall be reduced when accelerator pedal signals are inconsistent.
reference_mappings.reason: The test verifies torque reduction for inconsistent pedal signals.

reference_mappings.requirement_example: The restraint controller shall store diagnostic evidence after a deployment-related fault.
reference_mappings.reason: The reusable test provides related diagnostic storage evidence but may require deployment-specific validation.

reference_mappings.requirement_example: The battery system shall detect thermal runaway risk and store fault data.
reference_mappings.reason: The test provides reusable battery fault storage evidence for thermal-risk conditions.

reference_mappings.requirement_example: Cruise control shall allow immediate driver override during active control.
reference_mappings.reason: The test directly verifies driver override priority.

reference_mappings.requirement_example: The instrument cluster shall show high-priority safety warnings within the required latency.
reference_mappings.reason: The software-level test supports warning latency evidence, while physical display validation remains external.
